summary_one_line: Input must contain the literal ASCII string "Content-Type" so match_header succeeds and the header-search loop exits via its false side.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A header block containing the literal ASCII bytes "Content-Type" (43 6f 6e 74 65 6e 74 2d 54 79 70 65) so that search_header's match_header finds a matching header, sets value non-NULL, and the loop condition !value becomes false on the next iteration — taking the blocked false side at mime.c:350.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: All winner-resolving seeds carry the contiguous ASCII run "Content-Typ" at offsets 0x24-0x2f (47 43 6f 6e 74 65 6e 74 2d 54 79 70 = G C o n t e n t - T y p), the exact case-sensitive prefix curl searches for via STRCONST("Content-Type").
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds carry no "Content-Type" run; at the offsets where winners hold the literal (0x25-0x2c) the loser bytes diverge entirely (e.g. 80, 2e, fe, eb, ff), so match_header never matches, value stays NULL, and the loop only ever takes its true side.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog/vpc intercept the string CMP inside match_header (__sanitizer_weak_hook_strcmp/memcmp), record both operands, and I2SRandReplace substitutes the program-side constant "Content-Type" into the input at matching offsets. naive and value_profile lack I2SRandReplace and must hit the 12-byte case-sensitive literal by chance, which they never do across 10 trials.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the false side of the header-search loop at mime.c:350, which exits only when value becomes non-NULL.
value is set by match_header inside the loop body, so a successful header match drives the false-side exit.
The caller searches specifically for the literal "Content-Type" header name.
Winner-resolving seeds contain the contiguous ASCII "Content-Typ" run that loser seeds lack.
At offsets 0x25-0x2c the winner bytes are the case-sensitive Content-Type literal while loser bytes diverge entirely.
cmplog adds I2SRandReplace which substitutes the recorded CMP operand into the input at matching offsets.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
`I2SRandReplace` reads the post-execution `CmpLogObserver` buffer,
picks a CMP entry, scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: naive (the loser) has no I2S mutator and must find the literal by chance.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: **Observed `mutation_op` in seed metadata**: any of the above. No I2S.

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile (the other loser) also lacks I2SRandReplace.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 266 --role W --fuzzer value_profile_cmplog --trial 1 --seed 3ada62fdafca01cd` and observed 2 I2S-floor dash rows (mutation_op = -) at depths 18 and 19 of the 20-level ancestor chain — direct lower-bound evidence of an I2SRandReplace contribution in the winner's lineage.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating mime.c:350's false side on a 12-byte case-sensitive "Content-Type" strcmp in which naive/value_profile resolve the branch as readily as cmplog/vpc — i.e. the I2S advantage disappears, showing the literal is reachable without operand substitution.

weakest_evidence_point: The dash-row floor is a lower bound and the winning seed shown was discovered by a havoc-tagged leaf op, so I2S contribution is inferred from ancestor dash rows rather than from a directly I2S-tagged resolving mutation.
